steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence before the blank says Delacroix "challenges his own ideas, beliefs, and values" no matter the topic. The sentence after the blank says he "questions his own perspective, regardless of subject matter." These two sentences say the same thing in different words, so the transition must signal reinforcement or confirmation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: signals that what follows is a specific illustration of the previous point. But the next sentence restates the claim generally rather than giving a specific example. ✗
  • Choice B — At that time,: signals a time shift. Nothing in the passage shifts time frame — both sentences describe Delacroix's general habit. ✗
  • Choice C — Indeed,: signals confirmation — the following statement reinforces or strengthens the preceding one. Fits perfectly because the third sentence restates and affirms the second. ✓
  • Choice D — However,: signals contrast or contradiction. The third sentence does not contradict the second — they express the same idea. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
